text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, young Lucy strolled through an old oak forest that smelled vaguely of mushroom honey. The leaves were fiery reds and oranges, casting a golden glow on everything. She paused at a patch of owl nests.

Suddenly owl eyes flashed out; they were enormous owls! One stared at her hungrily.

"Not YOU this time," Lucy whispered.

She chuckled politely.

The owl hooted in friendly greeting.

She continued along the narrow path. Around hawk nests fluttered brown birds. She smiled at them.

"Why did YOU choose that nest?"

Just an old hawk mum. It was her mum.

Lucy pondered. "That's very caring."

She looked back. The owl was nursing—

"No YOU go."

She laughed heartily. And with that, off flew the tiny bird, possibly a baby hawk.

Back to wandering through ancient trees. She stumbled upon an old man.

"You're a strange girl."

"Worse. I talk Owl."

The man laughed heart
